title: Sum-of-the-Years'-Digits
event: Hollis Co. buys equipment for $130,000 with a $10,000 salvage value and a 5-year life. Compute depreciation for years 1 and 2 under sum-of-the-years'-digits.

computationPaths.narration: SYD front-loads with shrinking fractions: remaining life over the digit sum, applied to depreciable cost.

memorize.body: SYD = (cost − salvage) × remaining life ÷ [n(n+1)/2]. The numerator counts DOWN each year.
SYD vs DDB, the two accelerated methods: SYD subtracts salvage up front and uses fractions; DDB ignores salvage in the formula and uses book value.

questions.prompt: Cost $130,000, salvage $10,000, 5-year life. Year-1 SYD depreciation = ?

distractors.feedback: You applied 5/15 to the FULL 130,000 cost. SYD uses DEPRECIABLE cost (120,000): 40,000.
That's straight-line (120,000 ÷ 5). SYD front-loads year 1 with the 5/15 fraction: 40,000.
